title: Quiz: Generative Adversarial Networks Fundamentals
description: Test your understanding of GAN concepts and training

questions.question: What are the two main components of a GAN?
questions.explanation: A GAN consists of a Generator that creates fake samples and a Discriminator that tries to distinguish real from fake samples.

questions.question: What is the GAN training objective?

options.text: Minimax game between G and D

questions.explanation: GANs are trained as a minimax game: min_G max_D V(D,G) where G minimizes and D maximizes the value function.

questions.question: At Nash equilibrium, what should D(x) equal for all samples x?
questions.explanation: At equilibrium, the discriminator cannot distinguish real from fake, so D(x) = 0.5 for all samples, meaning it's just guessing.

questions.question: What is mode collapse in GAN training?

options.text: The discriminator collapses to always output 0
The generator produces limited variety of outputs
The training loss goes to infinity
The model runs out of memory

questions.explanation: Mode collapse occurs when the generator maps many different latent vectors to the same or very similar outputs, producing limited diversity.

questions.question: In the non-saturating GAN loss, the generator maximizes log(D(G(z))) instead of minimizing log(1 - D(G(z))).
questions.explanation: True. The non-saturating loss provides stronger gradients early in training when D(G(z)) is small, helping the generator learn more effectively.

questions.question: What does the Wasserstein GAN (WGAN) use instead of JS divergence?
questions.explanation: WGAN uses the Wasserstein (Earth Mover's) distance, which provides smoother gradients and better correlates with sample quality.

questions.question: Why is spectral normalization used in GANs?

options.text: To stabilize training by constraining the Lipschitz constant

questions.explanation: Spectral normalization constrains the spectral norm (largest singular value) of weight matrices, enforcing a Lipschitz constraint that stabilizes training.

questions.question: Which are common issues in GAN training? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Vanishing gradients when D is too good

questions.explanation: GANs commonly suffer from mode collapse, vanishing gradients when the discriminator becomes too strong, and training instabilities. Convergence is NOT guaranteed.
